                                        PRINCIPAL DIFFERENCES BETWEEN TWO SERIES OF DATA ON
                                                NONPARTICIPATION IN LABOR FORCE




                               Data obtained through regular                            Data obtained through special series of
                              interview of all nonparticipants                             questions asked of one-fourth of
                                     in CPS sample                                         nonparticipants in CPS sample


                    "Going to school"—Includes all the nonpartici-                   "In school"—In addition to all the nonparticipants
                    pants who report "going to school" as their                      who report "going to school" as their major
                    major activity during the reference week (ques-                  activity during the reference week (question 19),
                    tion 19).                                                        this group includes a small number of persons
                                                                                     who, in response to 24D, give "in school or other
                                                                                     training" as their reason for not seeking work.
                                                                                     As a result, the number of "in school" nonpar-
                                                                                     ticipants obtained through the additional ques-
                                                                                     tions asked of one-fourth of the CPS sample is
                                                                                     slightly higher (about 1 percent) than the number
                                                                                     obtained through the regular interview of the
                                                                                     entire sample.
                    "Unable to work"—-This group includes only                        "Ill health, disability"—This group includes all
                    those persons who are afflicted by a physical or                 persons who attribute their nonparticipation in
                    mental ailment or disability which is expected                   the labor force to ill health or disability, r e -
                    to prevent them from working for at least 6                      gardless of the intensity or expected duration of
                    months.                                                          the condition afflicting them. Consequently, the
                                                                                     number of nonparticipants placed in the "ill
                                                                                     health or disability .category" is about twice as
                                                                                     large as the number of nonparticipants classified
                                                                                      as "unable to work."

                     "Keeping house"—This group includes all those                   "Home responsibilities"—This category of non-
                     persons (mostly women) who report housekeep-                    participants is roughly synonymous to the "keep-
                     ing as their major activity during the reference                ing house" group obtained from the entire CPS
                     week.                                                           sampleo However, it excludes those persons
                                                                                     who, although having kept house during the ref-
                                                                                     erence week, cite an illness or disability as
                                                                                     the reasons for leaving their last job or as an
                                                                                     obstacle to current work-seeking, or who think
                                                                                     that it would be impossible for them to find a
                                                                                     job. Because of this, the average number of
                                                                                     persons placed in the "home responsibilities"
                                                                                     category runs about 6 to 7 percent lower than the
                                                                                     number classified as "keeping house."

                     ^ ^ § £ ^ § ^ 9 5 ^ ^ ~ " T n i s g r 0 U P includes all non-   "Retirement or old age"—The nonparticipants
                     participants whose activity during the reference                placed in this category through the additional
                     week does not fall within the three categories                  questions are part of those who fall within the
                     discussed above. (In the new data obtained from                 "other reasons" category in data collected from




Digitized for FRASER
http://fraser.stlouisfed.org/
Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis
                               Data obtained through regular              Data obtained through special series of
                              interview of all nonpar tic ipants             questions asked of one-fourth of
                                     in CPS sample                            nonparticipants in CPS sample


                   one-fourth of the CPS sample, this group           the entire sample. However, this group does
                   of nonparticipants is divided roughly into the     not include all persons who might consider
                   three groups listed in the opposite column:        themselves "retired" in terms of having per-
                   "Retirement or old age," "think cannot get job,"   manently separated themselves from their jobs.
                   and "all other reasons.")                          As used here, the retirement or old age concept
                                                                      applies, first of all, only to persons 60 years and
                                                                      over who have not worked during the previous
                                                                      5 years and to persons who, regardless of their
                                                                      age, have left their last job because of "retire-
                                                                      ment or old age." Even within these two groups,
                                                                      however, a person is not classified as retired
                                                                      if reported as either wanting a job, keeping house
                                                                      (which allows few women to be classified as
                                                                      retired), or if prevented from seeking work by
                                                                      an illness or disability.

                                                                       "Think cannot get job"—This category includes
                                                                      all nonparticipants who say they want a job(24C)
                                                                      but add that they think it would be impossible
                                                                      for them to find work (24D). These persons are
                                                                      often referred to as "discouraged workers."

                                                                                        l—This group includes all the
                                                                      nonparticipants who, even on the basis of their
                                                                      responses to the additional questions, cannot be
                                                                      placed in any of the five categories discussed
                                                                      above.

                                                                  Technical Note

                                                      Additional information concerning the preparation
                                                      of the labor force, employment, hours and earnings,
                                                      and labor turnover series—concepts and scope,
                                                      survey methods, and limitations—is contained in
                                                      technical notes for each of these series, available
                                                      from the Bureau of Labor Statistics free of charge.

                                                                       ATTENTION
                         As discussed in the Technical Note, the Bureau periodically adjusts the industry employment series to a recent
                   benchmark to improve their accuracy. These adjustments may also affect the hours and earnings series because employ-
                   ment levels are used as weights. Industry data for all national series shownin this report have been adjusted to March
                   1968 benchmarks. Data from April 1968 forward are subject to revision at the time of the next benchmark.

                        Beginning with the July 1969 and subsequent issues of Employment and Earnings, the national data in sections B,
                   C, andD supersede those published in previous issues, as well as those appearing in the Handbook of Labor Statistics,
                   1969. Comparable data will be published in Employment and Earnings, United States, 190 9-69, BLS Bulletin 1312-7.
